reasoning: The reference value for Malawi as of late October 2025 was 1.000, which indicates that there were virtually zero 'Riots' recorded over the preceding 360 days. To trigger a positive resolution, there would need to be more than 10 times this baseline (i.e., 11 or more distinct 'Riots') in the 30-day window leading up to February 7, 2026. In Malawi, a sudden explosion of >10 riots in a single month after a year of near-zero riots is extremely improbable. While general elections are scheduled for September 2025, the 30-day window ending in February 2026 is several months post-election. Any immediate post-election instability would likely resolve well before this window. Furthermore, ACLED's strict criteria for coding an event as a "Riot" (as opposed to a "Protest") means that even widespread demonstrations are unlikely to cross the 11+ threshold unless there is an unprecedented nationwide descent into violence. Therefore, the probability is set to the lowest bound.
